subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/ECF, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, TI, Freedom House) + UN (MONUSCO, Group of Experts, WHO) + HRW/Amnesty/ACLED + independent press.

A mineral superpower (world #1 cobalt, #2 copper) crippled by an active eastern war (Rwanda-backed M23 holds Goma/Bukavu), a catastrophic resource curse, and among the weakest states on earth; a 2025 disinflation/IMF print is the lone bright spot.
Standout drags: an active war in the east, a resource-curse trade model, near-worst infrastructure and healthcare, and pervasive corruption. Overall: clearly negative.

economic.notes: A mineral superpower undercut by a catastrophic resource curse, with a fragile 2025 disinflation.

fiscal_state.notes: Mineral-revenue-reliant with weak domestic revenue, on an IMF program, conflict-disrupted. (IMF reviews 2025-26)

monetary_independence.notes: A heavily dollarized franc with limited central-bank independence. (IMF Art IV 2026)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The franc is not held as reserves. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Fell sharply to ~2% in 2025 on franc appreciation, but with persistent FX fragility. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A mineral superpower but a textbook resource curse - artisanal/conflict mining, China-dominated refining, and smuggling that funds the war; rich but impoverished. (IMF; ACLED; PIIE)

institutional.notes: Pervasive corruption, a barely-governed state, and a pressured press.

rule_of_law.notes: A very low TI standing (~20/100), pervasive corruption, weak courts, and impunity. (TI CPI 2025; Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: Opaque, with large unmeasured conflict zones. (IMF data caveats)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Very weak and patronage-ridden, with roughly half the country roadless or ungoverned. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A falling RSF rank, with eastern stations shut, attacks, and killings. (RSF)

political_social.notes: A vast barely-governed state with an active eastern war and deep ethnic fractures.

political_stability.notes: A disputed 2023 re-election; a vast, barely-governed state with the east in active war. (Freedom House; Crisis Group)

civil_liberties.notes: Formal freedoms undercut by crackdowns and conflict-zone atrocities; rated 'Not Free'. (HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A conflict state both hosting and producing refugees - strained, not a destination. (UN/IOM)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep ethnic/regional fractures with 100+ armed groups. (HRW; ACLED)

demographics.notes: Huge, very young, and fast-growing - a dividend-or-strain double-edge. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Western-mineral-courted but unstable, with one of the world's deadliest active wars in the east.

alliance_reliability.notes: A 2025 US minerals-for-security partnership courts the West, but it is unproven and contested. (State Dept; PIIE)

conflict_involvement.notes: An active war in the east - Rwanda-backed M23 holds Goma/Bukavu - plus ADF/ISIS and 100+ militias, among the world's deadliest. (UN; ACLED; HRW)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Sanctions on commanders/armed-group figures and FX controls. (US Treasury; UN Group of Experts)

physical_practical.notes: Among the world's worst infrastructure and healthcare, with active disease outbreaks and conflict violence.

climate.notes: A Congo-basin carbon sink, but with flooding, deforestation, and disease. (World Bank/IEA)

healthcare.notes: Among the world's lowest spend, with simultaneous 2025 Ebola, mpox, cholera, and measles outbreaks. (WHO; Lancet)

infrastructure.notes: Among the world's worst - roughly half the country roadless and only ~20% electricity access despite hydropower potential. (World Bank; IEA)

crime_safety.notes: Conflict-zone violence, kidnapping, and executions, very unsafe in the east. (HRW; ACLED)

summary: A mineral superpower - the world's leading cobalt and a top copper producer - crippled by a catastrophic resource curse and an active war in its east, where Rwanda-backed M23 rebels seized Goma and Bukavu in 2025 amid 100+ militias and one of the world's deadliest conflicts. Institutions are among the weakest on earth (pervasive corruption, a barely-governed state roughly half of which is roadless), healthcare faces simultaneous Ebola/mpox/cholera outbreaks, and infrastructure and electricity access are near the world's worst. A 2025 disinflation under an IMF program is the lone, fragile bright spot.
